taskDescription: Drag the correct embryological terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph.
textField:
Human prenatal development spans approximately *38* weeks (266 days) from *fertilisation* to delivery. The gestational age is calculated from the last menstrual period, which is approximately *40* weeks.

The first two weeks of development are called the *all-or-none* period because exposure to teratogens either kills the embryo or causes no malformation. During this period, the zygote undergoes *cleavage* divisions forming a *morula*, which then develops a cavity to become the *blastocyst*.

The term *ontogeny* refers to the developmental history of an individual organism, while *phylogeny* refers to the evolutionary history of a species. The biogenetic law states that ontogeny *recapitulates* phylogeny, though this is now considered an oversimplification.

The period of greatest susceptibility to teratogens is the *embryonic* period (weeks 3-8) because this is when *organogenesis* occurs and all major organ systems are being established. The three primary germ layers formed during *gastrulation* are ectoderm, *mesoderm*, and endoderm.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Review the details you missed.
